body.title: NE arc rendering doubles thread-context lines (second input-echo surface)
body.result: NE deterministic-arc builder now pulls top 6 observations, substring-dedups on normalized form, keeps first 3 distinct. Matches Akien's exact pattern. 4 tests pass. Commit e65931c8. Igor restart to activate.
body.description:
Yesterday's T-igor-input-echo-thread-history (659bb5a4) fixed the thread-anchor write path — anchors no longer store recursively-prefixed user_input. But Akien's 2026-04-19 paste shows NE's own arc-rendering still duplicates context:

  [NE] arc: Igor is engaged with (positive): relationship: operator
  [Web message from akien]: hello; relationship: operator
  [Web message from akien]: hello
  TALKING WITH: Akien | re;

'[Web message from akien]: hello' appears twice in one arc line. This is a DIFFERENT surface from the thread_anchor write — it's NE's context assembly for arc / prompt construction. Same kind of duplication (two copies of same tag+content), different producer.

## Suspect paths
- narrative_engine.py _arc_build or equivalent — pulls from multiple memory sources (TWM + ring + relevant) and concatenates without dedup
- The TWM push path: thread.recent_user + thread.prior_user both end up in arc context without a de-dup pass
- reasoners/base.py _build_session_context — might be appending the same message via two paths (e.g. ring entry + TWM observation)

## First step
Log or pdb what the NE arc line is BUILT from — which memory IDs contribute. If the same (source, content) pair comes from two different observation IDs, that's the dedup gap.

## Acceptance
- No adjacent duplicate [Web message from X]: lines in NE arc output
- No regression in the parts of arc context that SHOULD repeat (some things are correctly repeated for salience; this ticket is about ACCIDENTAL duplication)

## Related
- T-igor-input-echo-thread-history (659bb5a4, done) — sibling surface, same kind of bug
